Title: Kangar local council seizes 400 units of liquid vape and 70 devices worth RM15,000
Article:
KANGAR: The Kangar Municipal Council seized 400 units of electronic cigarettes or vape liquid and 70 related devices, worth RM15,000, in an operation at a premises in Jalan Padang Behor here last night.
Its president, Affendi Rajini Kanth said the seizure, during the operation between 10.30pm and 1 am, in collaboration with the police, was carried out in accordance with the provisions of the MPK Trade, Business, and Industry Licensing By-Laws, in line with the Perlis state government's proclamation prohibiting the operation of vape sales premises in the state effective Aug 1, 2025.
"The premises in question has also been fined several times and a business cessation notice was issued in August 2025, yet it continues to operate the vape business," he said in a statement today.
He said that based on the provisions of the by-law, it imposes a fine not exceeding RM2,000 and an additional fine not exceeding RM200 for each day the offence continues after conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia aims to halve food imports by 2050
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The government aims to reduce Malaysia's dependence on imported food by up to 50 per cent by 2050 in a bid to curb food imports, which are currently estimated at about RM80 billion annually.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the target would be achieved in stages, beginning with a reduction of at least 15 per cent by 2030, followed by 30 per cent by 2040.
He said the effort would be driven by various food security initiatives implemented by the Rural and Regional Development Ministry and its agencies over the past three years.
"Our goal is to help the country reduce food imports by 50 per cent from the current level.
"By 2030, I believe we can reduce imports by at least 15 per cent. By 2040, the reduction should reach 30 per cent, and I believe we can achieve a 50 per cent reduction before 2050.
"Although this is a long-term programme, based on the successes we have already achieved, we have a clear projection to reduce food imports through agencies that are not even under the Agriculture and Food Security Ministry," he said at a press conference after attending the Johor RISDA Agromajestic Programme 2026 at Dataran Kejat, Bandar Tenggara, here today.
Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said one of the key strategies was to utilise underused or idle land owned by government agencies for high-impact agriculture and livestock projects, including broiler chicken, layer chicken and beef cattle farming.
He said the initiative would not only increase domestic food production but also optimise the use of existing government assets to strengthen the country's food security.
"Land that is underutilised or not fully utilised will be converted for various Agro Madani agricultural projects to enhance food security, as well as livestock farming.
"This is being carried out in collaboration with various parties, including the Department of Veterinary Services, Felcra and other agricultural agencies under the state governments and the ministry," he said.
He said cooperation between ministries and agencies would help accelerate efforts to boost local food production, thereby reducing the country's reliance on imported food supplies over the long term.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Import ban on 5 Thai shrimp species stays for now
Article:
KOTA BARU: The import ban on five aquaculture shrimp species from Thailand will remain pending the  Fisheries Department's evaluation of data from Thai authorities.
Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the assessment is expected to take at least 30 days, depending on the completeness of the data received from Thailand.
He said a technical team established by the department is scheduled to meet next week to review the second phase of information received before obtaining a more comprehensive overview of the country's compliance levels.
"For now, Malaysia is still maintaining the shrimp import ban from Thailand," he told a press conference at the East Zone 2026 myAgropreneur Perikanan (myAP) and myKomuniti Perikanan Carnival.
Adnan said the evaluation aims to ensure that all requirements related to biosecurity, disease control, and compliance with food safety standards are met.
On June 1, Malaysia put a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand.
The temporary ban involves Penaeus esculentus (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn), and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
Adnan said among the main factors for the restriction was to ensure all shrimp imported into the country are free from any diseases that could threaten the local aquaculture industry.
"Apart from that, we also want to ensure that the imported shrimp do not contain any residues or prohibited chemicals that violate national food safety regulations and standards," he said.
He said Malaysia also faced a similar situation as local shrimp exports to Thailand have been blocked since 2017.
Adnan said the government is now streamlining the domestic control system to ensure Malaysian shrimp meet health and food safety standards, thereby opening the way for re-export to Thailand.
"We want to ensure Malaysian shrimp are disease-free and do not contain any residues or prohibited chemicals so that they can re-enter the Thailand market," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN-PH enjoy very good ties at federal level, says Zahid
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional (BN) enjoys a very good relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, with cooperation between the two political coalitions remaining strong despite the heat of the Johor state election campaign.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the leadership of both BN and PH has consistently demonstrated professionalism and political maturity, placing the national agenda above all other considerations.
"Cooperation between BN and PH is very good. Let me confirm that it is indeed very good, and we remain highly professional.
"We distinguish between national issues, which we must address together as members of the Cabinet, and issues at the state level, for example," he said after launching the Risda Agro Majestic event in Bandar Tenggara today.
Commenting on the current Johor state election campaign, Zahid said both political coalitions remain committed to preserving harmony within the unity government by adopting a mature approach to election campaigning.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said the political strategies of both sides are based on healthy competition without resorting to excessive provocation.
"Among the approaches we have adopted is that we do not attack one another, even though we are contesting in the same arena, particularly in Johor. The same approach will apply in Negri Sembilan, but we will not attack below the belt.
"If anyone from either side slips up, we should de-escalate the situation. Most importantly, democracy must be upheld and practised in the best possible manner," he said.
He said the winning formula in today's political landscape no longer depends on a party's ability to attack or discredit its opponents. Instead, voters assess the quality and merits of the candidates put forward.
He cited as examples the healthy competition among candidates with professional backgrounds, including in the medical field, as well as former ministers.
"We should promote our respective candidates, such as Dr Naqib Ghazali in Panti and Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba in Pasir Raja, who is a medical specialist and a former health minister. These are the candidates we should be promoting, rather than attacking other parties or their candidates," he said.
A total of 172 candidates have been confirmed to contest the Johor state election, which is scheduled to be held on July 11, while early voting has been set for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Onn Abu Bakar outlines 6 pledges for Senggarang's development
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Senggarang state seat, Onn Abu Bakar, today unveiled his 'Six Pledges for a More Progressive and Prosperous Senggarang' manifesto for the upcoming 16th Johor state election.
He said the pledges were comprehensively formulated and reflected his commitment to ensuring the holistic development of Senggarang, covering the economy, infrastructure, education, welfare and the well-being of the people.
Onn said the first pledge focuses on providing quality internet access for all by advocating improved telecommunications coverage in housing estates, villages and rural areas to support education, business and daily activities.
"The second pledge is to enhance public safety through the installation of additional village street lights and the upgrading of existing ones in identified high-risk areas to improve road safety, reduce the risk of accidents and help prevent crime," he said during a live broadcast on Facebook today.
On infrastructure, under the third pledge, he said efforts to upgrade roads and implement flood mitigation measures, including improvements to drainage and irrigation systems in Senggarang, would be prioritised to address flash floods and the impact of tidal surges, which have long affected residents in several areas.
The fourth pledge aims to strengthen the local economy through training programmes, access to financing and marketing support, as well as new business opportunities for entrepreneurs, traders, fishermen, farmers and smallholders to help increase their incomes, said the Batu Pahat member of parliament.
On youth and education, Onn said the fifth pledge seeks to expand opportunities for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), digital education and artificial intelligence (AI) training to produce a highly skilled workforce that meets current industry demands.
Under the sixth pledge, he said he would strengthen the role of the people's service centre to make it more responsive, efficient and accessible, ensuring that senior citizens, persons with disabilities (PwDs) and single mothers continue to receive assistance and are not left out of government welfare programmes.
Onn said the manifesto he is offering is not merely a collection of empty promises but a sincere commitment and a comprehensive action plan aimed at improving the well-being of residents in the Senggarang state constituency.
He said Senggarang has strong economic potential, given its rich agricultural and fisheries resources, vibrant small-scale industries and harmonious community.
He added that he would continue to push for the upgrading of fishermen's jetties so that they can be recognised by the Fisheries Development Authority of Malaysia, while also seeking to simplify the process of obtaining coastal fishing licences from the Fisheries Department.
On the Johor state election polling day on July 11, Onn said a voter turnout of between 70 and 75 per cent would give him an advantage and pave the way for victory in Senggarang.
Early voting for the Johor state election will be held on July 7.
N24: SENGGARANG
: 38,576 voters
1. Datuk Mohd Rashid Hasnon (PN-Bersatu)
2. Onn Abu Bakar (PH-PKR)
3. Mohd Yusla Ismail (BN-Umno)

Title: Armed Forces to probe after 'distasteful' videos by 'soldier' go viral
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Armed Forces will investigate claims that a "soldier" has been uploading videos on social media accompanied by derogatory captions.
Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman said the military was aware of the posts, which have gone viral on Threads.
"We will investigate," he said when contacted, adding that the probe would determine the authenticity of the posts.
Earlier, a Threads user questioned the actions of the person behind the videos, which appear to show a man in military uniform accompanied by what were described as "distasteful" captions.
The post drew reactions from netizens, with many saying such actions could tarnish the image and reputation of the Malaysian Armed Forces.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stick insects becoming harder to find amid concerns over habitat change
Article:
KOTA BARU: Stick insects, famed for their twig-like camouflage, are believed to be increasingly rare in the wild, sparking concerns over a potential population decline linked to habitat and climate change.
The Head of the Department of Preclinical Veterinary Sciences at Universiti Malaysia Kelantan (UMK), Dr Goh Soon Heng, said that although there is no scientific data indicating the species is under threat of extinction, early signs suggest the insects are becoming less commonly found than before.
"From a scientific perspective, the data does not show that stick insects are currently at risk of extinction. However, local observations suggest they are becoming increasingly hard to find compared with the past.
"If they are becoming harder to find, it may be an early sign that their population is declining, although more data is needed to confirm this," he told Bernama recently.
[[nid:1480265]]
He said ecosystem changes, rising temperatures, and shifts in plant species in their natural habitats were among the factors that could threaten the insects' survival.
He said changes in plants not only reduce food sources, but also impair the insects' ability to blend in and escape predators.
"When tree species in a given area change, the habitat and natural shelter of stick insects are also disrupted, making them easier for predators such as birds to detect and eat," he said.
He said Malaysia is home to more than 100 species of stick insects, varying in shape and size, with some resembling twigs while others resembling leaf insects, adding that the insects are mainly distinguished by their long, thin, and tapering bodies, which closely resemble real twigs.
"In addition to their bodies, stick insects have long, delicate legs, which make them hard to tell apart from twigs in their natural habitat," he said.
He said most stick insect species live in the tree canopy, with their location depending on size; larger species are usually found lower down, while smaller or younger ones tend to inhabit higher branches.
He said stick insects are neither poisonous nor venomous, although some species have spines on their bodies for self-defence.
The insects require between 10 and 15 months to complete their life cycle, from egg to a mature adult capable of reproducing, he said.
"The eggs usually take between two and three months to hatch before the young insects grow into adults within eight to nine months."
Dr Goh said stick insects play a role in the ecosystem by helping control leaf density across different tree layers and serving as a food source for a range of other animals.
He said this ecological role makes stick insects a vital component in preserving the balance of forest ecosystems.
He said no major conservation initiatives exist specifically for stick insects in Malaysia, although some zoos are involved in limited breeding programmes for stick and leaf insects to support education and awareness.
He also observed increasing public interest in stick insects as exotic pets, but warned against sourcing them from illegal channels that could affect wild populations.
"If protected species are involved, owners must obtain the required licences and adhere to regulations set by the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan)," he said.
He hopes that further research will be undertaken to determine the true status of stick insect populations in the country and to strengthen long-term biodiversity conservation efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lee: Selangor MB's Johor investment comparison oversimplifies economic realities
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A caretaker Johor executive councillor has defended the state's economic performance after Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari claimed its investment inflows failed to generate enough local jobs.
Johor caretaker Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said data from federal agencies proved Johor's economic foundation remained solid.
Amirudin, who is PKR vice-president, said on the
campaign trail for the Johor polls
that the gap between investment growth and employment opportunities remained a concern.
Amirudin had said Selangor attracted RM83 billion in investments last year, creating 60,000 new jobs, compared to around 40,000 jobs created in Johor.
"With due respect, I believe this comparison oversimplifies how modern economies work," Lee told the New Straits Times.
He said Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) figures showed Johor recorded RM110 billion in approved investments in 2025, the highest in the country, involving 1,348 projects expected to generate 24,584 direct jobs.
He said between 2022 and 2025, Johor attracted more than RM272 billion in approved investments, creating over 78,000 direct jobs, excluding indirect employment across construction, logistics, engineering and related supply chains.
The Barisan Nasional leader also cited Department of Statistics data showing Johor posted the highest Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth in the country for two consecutive years, at 6.4 per cent in 2024 and 8.0 per cent last year.
He said Johor's strategy did not focus solely on job volume but on attracting high-value, capital-intensive industries.
These include semiconductors, artificial intelligence, advanced manufacturing, medical devices and data centres, which offer higher-skilled and better-paying employment opportunities.
Lee said this direction was aligned with the Johor Economic Transformation Programme, which comprises 83 catalytic projects and 32 strategic interventions aimed at creating more than 200,000 quality jobs between 2026 and 2030.
He added that initiatives such as productivity-linked wages and the Johor Talent Development Council were designed to ensure workers benefit directly from economic growth through higher skills and better income prospects.
Lee also questioned the focus of comparisons with other states, saying Johor's performance should be viewed within the context of its own economic trajectory.
"Johor is already Malaysia's top-performing state in terms of approved investments and GDP growth," he said, adding that continued collaboration between federal and state governments was key to sustaining momentum.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM: PUNB targets RM2.25bil financing, govt may boost support
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Bhd (PUNB) is targeting to approve up to RM2.25 billion in financing between 2026 and 2030 under its long-term R30 Strategic Framework to accelerate the growth of Bumiputera companies.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said PUNB has been performing well and indicated that the government is prepared to increase its support if the agency continues to deliver.
"They're doing well. If they do well, we will increase (financing approval).
"At the moment, PUNB is among the agencies implementing the priorities we have outlined, while working closely with other Bumiputera agencies," he said briefly after attending the SParK 2026: Business Transformation opening ceremony.
PUNB also announced a reduction in the financing rate for its Prosper Grow facility to as low as 3.5 per cent per annum.
The agency also unveiled three new financing programmes, namely, Prosper Grow Biz Express, Prosper Grow Fuel Up and Prosper Grow Auto Biz, aimed at improving access to financing and strengthening entrepreneurs' working capital.
The RM2.25 billion financing target will be achieved through PUNB's existing facilities — Prosper Grow, Prosper Great and Prosper Impact/Nova — alongside enhancements to programmes tailored to meet the evolving needs of entrepreneurs.
Held ahead of PUNB's 35th anniversary, SParK 2026 brought together PUNB Entrepreneur Partners, Bumiputera entrepreneurs, corporate leaders, industry players, digital ecosystem participants, development agencies and strategic partners for conferences, knowledge-sharing sessions, business exhibitions and sales activities.
The two-day event served as a platform for entrepreneurs to gain industry insights, build strategic networks, showcase their products and explore new business opportunities.
Since its establishment in 1991, PUNB has supported more than 15,500 Entrepreneur Partners through its financing and business development programmes, with total approved financing amounting to RM5.15 billion across various Bumiputera business sectors.
During the event, PUNB also exchanged memoranda of understanding with the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) and Malaysian Technology Development Corp Sdn Bhd (MTDC) to strengthen entrepreneur development through greater use of data, technology and innovation.
The collaborations are expected to improve PUNB's planning and evaluation of entrepreneur development programmes while creating more opportunities for entrepreneurs in technology, innovation and commercialisation.
SParK 2026 also saw five PUNB Entrepreneur Partners receive the SParK 2026 Entrepreneur Awards in recognition of their achievements, resilience and business discipline in building sustainable enterprises, creating employment, expanding market reach and demonstrating strong business leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor campaign opens quietly as parties shift to targeted voter outreach
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The first week of campaigning in the 16th Johor state election has seen relatively moderate momentum, with all parties opting to engage voters more targeted rather than holding large-scale rallies.
Political analyst Prof Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian said all parties are adapting their campaign methods to the current political environment through more personal approaches, including house visits, small group meetings and community programmes.
He said such strategies not only give candidates the opportunity to meet voters face-to-face and understand their concerns, but also help parties strengthen their machinery and make better use of campaign resources ahead of a more critical second phase.
"The first week is mainly about building the foundations of the campaign.
This targeted campaign approach actually gives candidates an advantage in reinforcing their position in the constituencies they are contesting," the Senior Lecturer in Political Sociology at Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) told Bernama.
He said all party machinery has yet to reveal its real strength, and the campaign is expected to become more heated in the second week with senior leaders joining in, alongside more rallies and digital efforts targeting undecided voters.
Sharing a similar view, geostrategist Dr Azmi Hassan said modern election campaigns are increasingly data-driven, with parties also adopting hybridstrategies that heavily leverage social media platforms.
"In the past, campaigns were typically held on a large scale in open areas, but now campaign teams are deployed for direct face-to-face engagement to persuade voters, categorised as white, grey and black (supporters, neutrals and opposition)," said the Senior Fellow at the Nusantara Academy for Strategic Research (NASR).
Meanwhile, Ilham Centre researcher Mujibu Abd Muis said that, so far, campaign narratives from all contesting parties have largely focused on three main themes, namely, track records, future pledges, and efforts to convince voters that each party can deliver political stability.
The Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) political science lecturer said, however, that these narratives have not yet formed a dominant theme strong enough to reshape the campaign landscape as a whole.
"Ultimately, the impact of any narrative depends on whether it can be turned into issues that resonate with voters' daily lives, including the cost of living, employment prospects, local development and public service standards," he said.
He said the first week also saw most of the party machinery concentrating its campaigning in northern Johor, covering districts such as Muar, Tangkak and Segamat, as well as parts of Batu Pahat and Kluang.
"In my opinion, this reflects how parties are optimising their campaign resources by prioritising constituencies regarded as competitive or likely to be decisive.
"Northern Johor has several constituencies expected to be closely contested, so the presence of national leaders is not merely to draw attention but also to signal their strategic importance to each party," he said.
Meanwhile, Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Associate Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities director Professor Dr Mazlan Ali said the campaign activity in Johor has so far largely focused on a "contest" between the two main coalitions, Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH).
Mazlan said PH is seen as having greater influence in critical areas such as southern and western Johor, while BN is expected to perform strongly in the eastern coastal districts, especially Mersing and Kota Tinggi.
"So, the way campaign machinery is mobilised is closely connected to how each coalition assesses its likelihood of winning in different areas," he said.
Meanwhile, all analysts agreed that parties must focus on persuading voters to turn out and cast their ballots, noting that voter turnout will have a major impact on the final outcome of the Johor polls.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting 56 state assembly seats in the state election, with polling day set for July 11 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tonton names 223 winners in first round of 'Non Stop, On Top!' campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tonton has announced 223 winners in the first round of its 'Tonton Non Stop, On Top!' campaign, although the identity of the grand prize winner, who will drive home a brand-new TQ Wuling Bingo EV, has yet to be revealed.
The grand prize winner will be announced during a prize presentation ceremony on July 7.
Tonton said winners in the runner-up, consolation and special consolation categories have already been announced, with 17 Oppo smartphones and 280 cash vouchers awarded across the three categories.
Following the conclusion of the first round, the second round of the campaign began on June 29 and will run until July 12.
Under the campaign mechanics, all points accumulated in the previous round have been reset to zero, giving participants an equal opportunity to compete for a fresh set of prizes.
To earn points, TontonUp subscribers must stream the following selected Korean titles on the Tonton platform during the campaign period:

Tonton said the leaderboard reset gives both existing and new subscribers another opportunity to compete for premium rewards.

Title: Woman found drowned after fall from bridge in Dungun
Article:
DUNGUN: A woman who fell from the Sungai Paka bridge along Jalan Jabor-Paka near Bandar Al Muktafi Billah Shah yesterday was found drowned today.
District police chief Supt Nik Ab Halim Nik Mat said the body of Nor Haslini Din, 47, who had a history of mental illness, was found floating by the search and rescue team at 10.30am.
He said the woman was reported to have fallen from the 45-metre-high bridge at about 10.30am yesterday.
"The woman's body was found floating after becoming lodged against tree branches about 200 metres from the spot where she was reported to have fallen," he told Bernama when contacted today.
He said the woman's body was recovered after the search and rescue (SAR) operation resumed at 9am today.
He added that today's SAR operation involved 38 personnel from various agencies, as well as the public, and the victim's body was later taken to Dungun Hospital for a post-mortem examination.
Meanwhile, the victim's father, Din Zakaria, 77, said he was devastated that he was unable to save his daughter after she fell from the bridge, which is located about 100m from his home.
He said he rushed to the bridge after seeing his daughter, the youngest of his six children, fall into the river.
"I could only watch as Nor Haslini struggled in the water before being swept away by the strong current and disappearing beneath the surface," he said when met at the scene today.
He also expressed gratitude that his daughter's body had been found.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Crypto mining operation busted in Klang raid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have shut down an illegal digital currency mining operation at a warehouse in Port Klang Free Zone (PKFZ), Pulau Indah, Klang, following a raid yesterday.
South Klang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said the raid, conducted at about 9.30am, involved personnel from the Criminal Investigation Department in collaboration with Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) technicians and PKFZ auxiliary police.
"During the raid, two men, including a foreign national aged 20 and 31, were detained.
"All equipment believed to have been used in the digital currency mining activity was also seized to assist investigations," he said in a statement.
He said police are still investigating how long the mining activity had been operating at the warehouse.
A case has been opened under Section 427 of the Penal Code for mischief and Section 37(1) of the Electricity Supply Act.
Offences under Section 427 of the Penal Code carry a maximum jail term of up to five years or a fine, while Section 37(1) of the Electricity Supply Act provides for jail term of up to five years, a fine of up to RM100,000, or both upon conviction.
"Both suspects have been remanded for four days from today until July 7 to assist further investigations," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ramanan: HR Ministry to ensure jobs match qualifications, offer better wages
Article:
PASIR GUDANG: The Human Resources Ministry will ensure that every job created not only meets market demand but also offers higher wage value and matches jobseekers' qualifications.
Its minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the ministry's focus has shifted from merely creating large numbers of jobs to ensuring job quality aligns with applicants' skills.
He said the ministry has a mature labour market ecosystem through its comprehensive MYFutureJobs platform, which is powered by artificial intelligence (AI) to improve job matching accuracy.
"It is useless if we create job opportunities that are not decent, not well-paid, and not suitable for jobseekers.
"That is why we have MYFutureJobs. More than 300,000 job applications have been recorded, with 200,000 successfully matched, while over 100,000 job vacancies are still available on the platform," he told reporters at the Sree Maha Mariamman Temple today.
He said MYFutureJobs uses AI to generate job matches based on applicants' qualifications.
Yesterday, PH launched its Johor state election manifesto themed "Johor for All", outlining 10 key offers covering healthcare, housing, entrepreneurship, education, agriculture, transport and employment.
Among the pledges is a target to create 250,000 high-paying and decent jobs in Johor through the development of modern and high-value industries.
The target will be achieved by creating 50,000 jobs annually, together with efforts to raise Johor's median wage by at least 30 per cent.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting 56 state seats, with polling on July 11 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] End political patronage in financing Bumiputera entrepreneurs, says Anwar
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for an end to political patronage in the disbursement of financing to Bumiputera entrepreneurs.
Anwar said loans and financial assistance should be awarded based on capability and commitment, rather than personal connections or recommendation letters.
He said the long-standing practice of relying on political endorsements had weakened Bumiputera development institutions and resulted in public funds being channelled to people with little interest in building sustainable businesses.
"I do not care whether recommendation letters come from division chiefs, branch leaders or other influential figures.
"What matters is whether the entrepreneur has the capability. That is who we should help," he said at the opening of SParK 2026: Business Transformation.
Anwar said financing should no longer be influenced by what he described as "yellow, green or blue letters", referring to recommendation letters from politicians or influential individuals.
"These practices have damaged our Bumiputera institutions for far too long.
People received loans because of their close ties to certain individuals, not because they were capable entrepreneurs.
"In the end, the businesses collapsed because those receiving the assistance were never truly committed."
Anwar also criticised past abuses involving government financing, claiming that some recipients prioritised lavish offices, expensive vehicles and personal lifestyles instead of growing their businesses.
"In some cases, the first thing they did after receiving financing was set up luxurious offices, buy vehicles and even take a second wife.
"Others simply sold their business licences for commissions. This has to stop," he said.
Anwar said that genuine business failures should not be treated the same as deliberate abuse of public funds.
He said entrepreneurship inevitably involved risks and that even successful startup ecosystems overseas experienced high failure rates.
"We should not punish entrepreneurs who genuinely failed after making an honest effort. Give them another opportunity.
"But that is very different from giving loans to people who lacked the interest, experience or commitment from the beginning and then abused the assistance."
He said agencies should move away from measuring success solely by the amount of financing disbursed and instead assess whether entrepreneurs demonstrated transparency, commitment and the ability to build viable businesses.
"The measure is not simply how much money has been disbursed.
"What matters is whether the entrepreneurs are sincere, transparent and serious about making their businesses succeed," he said.
Anwar also urged agencies involved in Bumiputera entrepreneurship development, including Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Bhd, Majlis Amanah Rakyat and Tekun Nasional, to strengthen coordination and avoid duplicating programmes.
"The Bumiputera agenda can only succeed if it is managed properly and if we put an end to stealing and abuse. On that, I will not compromise," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: More Bumiputera agencies not the answer to advancing community's agenda
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government has no plans to set up new Bumiputera agencies but will instead strengthen existing institutions and improve their coordination to better support entrepreneurs.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said creating more agencies was not the answer to advancing the Bumiputera economic agenda.
"I am not inclined to establish new Bumiputera agencies. Instead, I want to strengthen the existing agencies," he said at the SParK 2026: Business Transformation opening ceremony.
He said better coordination was needed between government agencies, departments, and ministries.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mohamad Sabu leads Malaysian delegation in paying final respects to Khamenei [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu led the Malaysian government delegation in paying final respects to the late former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, at the Grand Mosalla in Teheran.
In a Facebook post today, Mohamad said the mourning ceremonies would take place over several days, beginning in Teheran before continuing to Qom, Najaf, and Karbala, before the remains are taken to the Imam Reza Shrine in Mashhad for burial on July 9.
He said the tribute and mourning ceremonies were being held more than 100 days after Khamenei was killed in an attack involving the United States and Israel on Feb 28.
"Alhamdulillah, the final respect for the late former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, at the Grand Mosalla has just concluded. Upon my arrival, I was welcomed by the Speaker of the Iranian Parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi," he said.
Mohamad also prayed that Allah SWT would shower His mercy upon the deceased, forgive his sins, and grant him a place among the righteous. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police rule out drugs after clip of driver's erratic behaviour goes viral
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A man seen behaving erratically after an accident on the Inner Ring Road was not driving under the influence of drugs, police said.
Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said the man's family had informed investigators that he had previously undergone psychiatric treatment.
"Medical records confirmed that he has been receiving treatment since April last year," Azrul said in a statement.
The 8.30am accident involved a Perodua Myvi driven by the 25-year-old man and a Proton X90 driven by a 31-year-old engineer.
The Proton X90 was slowing down to make a U-turn when the Myvi rear-ended it.
Azrul said neither driver was injured, but the Myvi driver began behaving erratically and aggressively after the crash.
He was later taken to Sultanah Aminah Hospital for treatment.
The case is being investigated under Rule 10 of the Road Traffic Rules for failing to maintain proper control of a vehicle, resulting in an accident.
Azrul urged anyone who witnessed the incident to assist the investigation by contacting the Johor Baru (South) Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division at 07-222 1722.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump vows to defend American identity in patriotic Mount Rushmore speech
Article:
MOUNT RUSHMORE, United States: US President Donald Trump lashed out Friday at what he called a fresh threat against the country's identity, taking aim at domestic "radicals and extremists" on the eve of America's 250th birthday.
In a speech dripping with patriotism, Trump lauded American exceptionalism and praised its past leaders, speaking at Mount Rushmore with its giant carved heads of four of his legendary predecessors.
But he added at the foot of the iconic monument: "As we approach this magnificent anniversary, we see our American identity under a renewed attack."
He charged that there was "a resurgence of the communist menace in our land."
It is a theme that Trump has repeatedly hammered home in recent weeks, as the anti-establishment left of the Democratic Party carried a surge of US primary victories.
The president has cast the rise of the left ahead of November's midterm elections that "communists" are on the rampage, posing a major "threat" to the country.
- 'UNDENIABLE ATTEMPT' -
Trump said Friday: "In recent years there's been an undeniable attempt to change this exceptional character, to beat the American spirit out of us, alienate us from our history."
While his language fell short of the more violent anti-immigrant rhetoric he has regularly wielded in past speeches, the underlying message was clear enough.
"You do not have to be born here, but you do have to love what we have built," he said.
The location of Trump's speech – a landmark upon which many suspect he would like to see his own face carved into rock – strikes a fitting image for a president who views himself as being one of the greats.
Trump's supporters have even introduced legislation to have his likeness chiseled beside those of George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Abraham Lincoln and Theodore Roosevelt.
At every stage, the 80-year-old Republican has sought to turn the United States' big anniversary into a celebration of himself.
On July Fourth, Trump will hold a campaign-style political rally on the National Mall in Washington, along with flyovers by military jets and what he has billed as the world's biggest fireworks display.
For Trump – the billionaire former reality TV star who became the consummate political showman – taking centre stage is in his blood.
- 'REALLY LONG SPEECH' -
But America under Trump is deeply divided at what should be a time of national unity.
Trump's approval ratings are near historic lows, fuelled largely by the war in Iran and the skyrocketing cost of living. His Democratic opponents have castigated him over his immigration policies, family's growing wealth and attempts to expand presidential power.
Trump's attempts to stamp his political brand on America's 250th anniversary have, meanwhile, not always gone to plan.
A Trump-linked organisation called Freedom 250 has taken control of many of the festivities from the bipartisan America250 group, leading many people to stay away from key events.
A "Great American State Fair" in the capital designed as a celebration of the country's birthday has drawn sparse crowds and widespread mockery for its empty booths.
A record-breaking heat wave also kept many visitors away, although Trump declared himself undeterred by the heat.
"On July 4 it's going to be approximately 41C out, and I'm going to go and I'm going to make a really long speech – just to show that I can do anything," he said Wednesday.
As the United States marks two-and-a-half centuries of triumphs and tragedies, slavery and freedom, civil war and world wars, surveys show the nation is pessimistic about the chances of realising the "American Dream."
A recent Quinnipiac University Poll showed 61 per cent of Americans thought the US was not living up to the ideals stated in the Declaration of Independence – though opinion on that was divided, with most Republicans thinking it did, and most Democrats thinking it did not.
"The Fourth of July really is a moment of freedom, but I have to be honest, in these political times, it hasn't been as exciting to me," Amy Kimaara, a 49-year-old special education teacher, told AFP in Los Angeles.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Johoreans need smarter, bolder elected representatives
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor voters need leaders who can wisely assess priorities, make brave decisions, and truly understand the public's needs, said Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Anwar, who is also prime minister, said the coalition would continue to champion the people's voice, defend the state's interests, and drive Johor toward greater development.
He said this in a video clip on his official Facebook page in conjunction with the 16th Johor state election.
Anwar also called on the PH machinery to work hard to ensure the victory of the party's candidates.
"We want to elect elected representatives and leaders who represent the interests of the people in Johor. The people of Malaysia, Malays, Chinese, Indians work together, build this nation, and raise this state to be great," he said.
He began PH's first day of campaigning by attending seven programmes across several areas to reach out to the electorate and support the coalition's candidates.
The events included community meetings, talks, and social gatherings aimed at strengthening voter support ahead of polling day.
PH has fielded candidates in all 56 state constituencies, comprising 20 from PKR, 19 from Amanah, and 17 from DAP. Overall, a total of 172 candidates are contesting the 56 state seats.
Early voting is set for July 7, while polling day will take place on July 11. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: BN learning from past to better serve people, says KJ
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional's lacklustre performance in the previous general elections forced the coalition to reassess its weaknesses.
It has since adopted a new approach aimed at rebuilding public trust and support.
Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said the nearly eight years since BN lost power had allowed the coalition to reassess its shortcomings and present a better offer to the people.
He said BN acknowledged that it was not perfect, and that the experience of being out of government and no longer leading the national administration had been an important lesson.
"BN is not perfect. We lost before, and we were punished by the people.
"But after nearly eight years of being sometimes outside, sometimes inside, but not leading the government, I think we have had time to reflect.
"There has been time for introspection, to look inward at our weaknesses, and we are now ready to offer something new to the people," he said when speaking at the Santai Petang Bersama KJ programme here yesterday.
Meanwhile, Khairy said Johor serves as an example that a government without a coalition arrangement can move faster and make decisions more effectively.
"I have no issue with coalitions, but when we form coalitions, decision-making tends to be slower. That is normal when we have partners in government who may come from different parties.
"So Malaysia is in a position where we need a stable and strong government like Johor.
"When BN governed Johor without a coalition, it ranked number one. Work and decisions were made quickly," he said.
Khairy also said the Johor election has become a unique 'test case' to evaluate the effectiveness of BN's approach in contesting solo before the same strategy is considered in Negri Sembilan, Melaka or even the next general election.
"I am confident the BN leadership will make decisions from time to time according to the situation, but Johor is a unique test case because we are able to contest solo here.
"After this, we will conduct a post-mortem. Whether we win or lose, win big or small, we will analyse the advantages of going solo or any weaknesses that may come with it.
"So this is a very good testing ground for the upcoming state elections and general election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: PN unlikely to unveil manifesto, to focus on targeted offer
Article:
PASIR MAS: Perikatan Nasional (PN) is unlikely to unveil a formal manifesto for the upcoming Johor state election, opting instead to present targeted offers tailored to the needs of voters in the constituencies it will contest.
PN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the coalition's approach reflects the limited number of seats it is expected to contest and its intended role as a credible opposition in the Johor state assembly.
"Traditionally, a manifesto is linked to the number of seats a party contests and whether it is seeking to form the government. Our party president has also touched on this.
"We will play the role of a credible opposition. In that context, I do not think there is a need for the party to present a manifesto as though we are taking over the Johor government," he told reporters here today.
Tuan Ibrahim, who is also Pas deputy president, said Perikatan Nasional would contest only 11 of the 56 state seats in Johor.
"As things stand, we will only be contesting 11 out of the 56 seats. Our priority is to carry out our role in the state legislative assembly by providing effective checks and balances on the state administration," he said.
Instead of a broad election manifesto, Tuan Ibrahim said the coalition would introduce several constituency-specific offers aimed at addressing the concerns and needs of local voters.
"There will be several offers for specific areas. We are taking into account the needs of the people and how to resolve issues in those constituencies.
"Perikatan Nasional has conducted several analyses and identified areas of focus, but it is not yet time to package them into a manifesto," he said.
He said Johor has long been regarded as an Umno stronghold, making PN's role as an effective opposition all the more important.
"Our priority is to ensure we can provide meaningful checks and balances in the Johor state assembly," he said.
There are 56 seats up for grabs in the Johor election, with 29 needed for a simple majority to form a state government.
Pas is contesting 11 seats, Bersatu 16, the Malaysian Indian People's Party five, and Pejuang one. Parti Wawasan Negara and Gerakan are not contesting the state polls.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nga: Madani govt on right track to boost Johor development
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is on the right track under the Madani government, with strong cooperation between the federal and Johor state governments seen as key to accelerating development and economic growth in the state, said DAP deputy chairman Nga Kor Ming.
In a statement, he said Johor stands to benefit further when both levels of government are administered by the same coalition with a shared vision and closer collaboration.
"Close collaboration between both levels of government has enabled development plans, infrastructure projects and public initiatives to be implemented more efficiently for the benefit of the people," he said.
Nga, who is also housing and local government minister, said a stable relationship between the federal and state governments would help boost economic growth, attract more investments and create better job opportunities for local communities.
He cited the RM110 billion in investments secured by the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) in Johor last year.
He added that closer coordination would further position Johor towards sustainable and inclusive development.
Nga said the Madani government remains committed to supporting Johor through continued investment, quality public services and people-centric policies aimed at improving living standards.
He said political stability, policy consistency and strong cooperation are essential to sustaining investor confidence and ensuring long-term prosperity.
Among the achievements highlighted were Malaysia's position as a preferred destination for foreign investors, supported by clear policies, strong fundamentals and stability, which attracted RM426.7 billion in foreign direct investment (FDI) in 2025.
He also noted the country's resilient trade performance, with Malaysia recording a trade volume of RM3.1 trillion in 2025 despite global economic uncertainties.
Nga further pointed to Malaysia's improved ranking in the Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI), rising from 67th to 54th place, as well as stronger international credit ratings following Moody's upgrade of Malaysia's outlook to A3 stable.
He also highlighted Malaysia's diplomatic engagements, including a RM52.73 billion strategic partnership with Turkmenistan and long-term energy cooperation with Russia, which he said would help secure the country's oil and gas supply for the next 20 years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Assistant skipper dies after saving passengers in boat tragedy
Article:
KUALA NERUS: A part-time boat crew member died after saving several passengers when a squid-jigging boat capsized and sank off Kuala Nerus last night.
The deceased, Ahmad Nasaruddin Mohmad Jalil, 37, managed to pull several people to safety before losing consciousness.
The incident occurred about 9 nautical miles from Dataran Kuala Nerus at 9pm, leaving one dead, three injured, and eight others safe.
The victim's eldest brother, Ahmad Firdaus Mohmad Jalil, 47, said Nasaruddin worked part-time during the squid-jigging season over the past two years to supplement his income.
"The deceased worked as a technician but he was a skilled boatman and was used to going out to sea. When the squid-jigging season arrived, he would take up part-time work as an assistant skipper with our cousin who worked as a fisherman.
"I was told that the deceased managed to save several other passengers before his strength ran out and he was taken onto another boat in an unconscious state. Yesterday evening I spoke to him as usual, nothing was amiss," Firdaus said when met at the Forensic Medicine Department of Sultan Zainal Abidin Hospital today.
Firdaus said his brother's remains would be taken to the Al-Abrar Mosque for funeral prayers before burial at the Kampung Bukit Tok Beng Muslim cemetery after the Zohor prayer.
Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said two of the three injured victims were treated as outpatients at Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital, while the third was still receiving follow-up treatment.
Azli said the injured passengers were Faris Ibrahim, 33; Ahmad Danial Iman, 24; and Fatin Fariesya Rohaizan, 26. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN: Jho Low must face justice here even if US pardons him
Article:
PASIR MAS: Perikatan Nasional has demanded the extradition of fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, to face justice in Malaysia regardless of any pardon granted to him by the United States.
"Jho Low must be extradited to Malaysia. We have our own laws, and the country has been severely affected by his actions," said PN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man.
"We will continue to insist that he be brought before the courts here," he said, adding that Low, the mastermind behind the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal, must still be given a fair trial.
Tuan Ibrahim said Malaysia's position should not be influenced by legal developments in the US, including the possibility of a presidential pardon.
"We have nothing to do with the United States' legal process. What matters is that offences allegedly committed in Malaysia must be dealt with under Malaysian law," he said on the sidelines of the Pasir Mas Pas annual general meeting.
He welcomed efforts by the US authorities to return assets linked to the 1MDB scandal to Malaysia, but said this was not enough and that Low must face justice here.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia would not alter its stand on Low even if the fugitive businessman were granted a presidential pardon in the US.
Anwar previously said the government was not considering the possibility of granting a pardon to Low.
It was recently reported that Low could be among those being considered for clemency by US President Donald Trump.
Low faces multiple charges, including corruption and money laundering, in the US and Malaysia over his alleged role in the misappropriation of 1MDB funds.
He has consistently denied any wrongdoing, while his whereabouts remain unknown.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Home Ministry allocates over RM429mil to strengthen enforcement agencies, personnel welfare in Johor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Home Ministry has allocated more than RM429 million since 2023 to strengthen the welfare of personnel and enhance the operational capabilities of enforcement agencies in Johor.
Its minister, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, said the allocation covers three key agencies, namely the police, Immigration Department and Prisons Department.
He said investing in personnel welfare goes beyond providing better facilities, describing it as a strategic effort to ensure enforcement officers can carry out their duties more efficiently, safely, and effectively in safeguarding public security.
"When enforcement personnel have a more conducive workplace, comfortable quarters, and modern facilities, the public also benefits through improved service delivery and a safer, more secure environment.
"The ministry will ensure that funds are used optimally to safeguard the welfare of personnel while strengthening the security of the people of Johor," he said in a post on his official Facebook page today.
He said the allocation comprised RM174.8 million for projects that have been completed or are currently under implementation, and RM255 million for projects in the planning stage.
Projects currently undergoing implementation include the acquisition of land for the Pengerang district police headquarters, the purchase of office premises and quarters for the Johor Baru Immigration Department, and the upgrading of basic facilities at Kluang Prison.
Those in the pipeline include the construction of the Segamat district police headquarters, which will involve a police station and residential quarters, the consolidation of the bus passenger terminal at the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), and upgrades to the kitchen workshop at Kluang Prison, as well as the water supply system at Simpang Renggam Prison.
Saifuddin Nasution said the initiatives reflect the Madani government's commitment to ensuring every state receives the support it requires based on its development priorities and the well-being of its people.
He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had recently
clarified
in the Dewan Rakyat that Johor's development and management allocation had increased significantly to RM14.6 billion, compared with RM10.2 billion previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HR officer, 80 foreigners detained in Johor illegal hiring probe [WATCH]
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Immigration Department detained 80 foreigners and a Malaysian human resources officer in raids on two factories in the Larkin industrial area here yesterday.
The 9am joint operation saw Johor Immigration and the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) targeting a component manufacturing factory and a snack production facility.
The human resources officer at one of the factories was also detained to assist investigations.
Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the raids were conducted following several weeks of surveillance after public tip-offs that the factory operators had allegedly hired undocumented foreign workers to reduce labour costs.
"The 80 foreign workers were detained for various immigration offences, including suspected illegal employment, failure to produce valid documents, and breaches under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963.
"Those detained comprised Pakistanis, Bangladeshis, Myanmar nationals and Indonesians, including 13 women," he said in a statement today.
All detainees have been placed at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMA calls for dedicated Health Ministry division for private primary care
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry should establish a dedicated division to develop and strengthen the country's private primary healthcare sector, the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) said.
Its president Datuk Dr R. Arasu said private general practitioner (GP) clinics are currently governed mainly through a regulatory framework, but a dedicated mechanism is needed to drive the sector's growth and improve the quality of care.
"While regulation is important, we also need a dedicated mechanism to enhance standards of care, support service development and strengthen private primary healthcare as a strategic partner in the national healthcare system," he told Berita Harian.
He was commenting on a front-page report by Berita Harian yesterday, which quoted the Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) as saying that many patients treated at hospitals could instead be managed at GP clinics, helping to reduce unnecessary hospital utilisation.
APHM secretary-general Anwar Anis said efforts to control healthcare costs should begin with strengthening primary care as the healthcare system's gatekeeper, rather than focusing solely on hospital costs.
Echoing the view, Dr Arasu said the government should significantly expand public-private collaboration by making greater use of the country's existing network of GP clinics.
He said services such as non-communicable disease (NCD) screening programmes, including PeKa B40, could be expanded to include chronic disease management, immunisation services, pre-employment medical examinations and suitable green-zone emergency cases.
"These services can be managed safely by GP clinics.
"This will immediately ease congestion at government hospitals and health clinics, allowing hospitals to focus on patients with more complex medical conditions while reducing the burden on an already overstretched public healthcare workforce," he said.
Dr Arasu also called for the acceleration of healthcare digitalisation, saying secure and interoperable electronic medical records would enable GPs, specialists and hospitals to communicate more effectively.
He said this would improve continuity of care, reduce unnecessary duplication of tests and ultimately lead to better patient outcomes.
In addition, he urged healthcare payers, including insurance companies and employer-sponsored healthcare programmes, to reinforce the role of GPs as patients' first point of contact before referrals are made to specialists when clinically necessary.
"This approach is not intended to restrict access to specialists but to ensure patients receive the right treatment, at the right place and at the right time," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reckless, dangerous driving cases surge nearly 50pct, say police
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  Cases of reckless and dangerous driving have increased by nearly 50 per cent in the first six months of this year compared to the same period last year.
The increase reflects a worrying trend of aggressive driving, which could lead to road bullying or road rage incidents.
Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said 1,278 investigation papers were opened from Jan 1 to June 28 this year, compared to 852 cases in the same period last year, an increase of 426 cases.
Johor recorded the highest number of cases this year with 354, followed by Kuala Lumpur (230), Selangor (200), Negri Sembilan (119), Kelantan (84), Perak (60), Terengganu (49), Sarawak (44), Melaka (37), Kedah (33), Penang (27), Pahang (24), Sabah (15) and Perlis (2).
Compared to the same period last year, Johor also recorded the highest increase with 175 cases, followed by Selangor (+117), Kuala Lumpur (+74), Negri Sembilan (+56), Terengganu (+26), Perak (+20), Sarawak (+11), Kedah (+8) and Pahang (+3), while Kelantan remained unchanged at 84 cases.
Penang, Sabah, Perlis and Melaka recorded declines.
"The main causes of road bullying include non-compliance with traffic rules and signals, aggressive driving such as dangerous overtaking, cutting in, tailgating and failure to give way," he told Bernama recently.
Other contributing factors include provocation between road users through excessive horn use, hand gestures or verbal disputes, as well as impatience and a lack of courtesy on the road.
Responding to claims on social media that some drivers use vape laced with drugs while driving, he said police acknowledged a trend involving vape products mixed with psychoactive substances or synthetic drugs.
He said enforcement efforts are being strengthened, including proposals to use saliva test kits and mandatory urine screening for commercial vehicle drivers involved in road accidents to detect drug influence.
Hasbullah said police takes firm action against road rage incidents, where offenders may be arrested and charged if the case involves threats, fights, property damage or injury.
He said reckless and dangerous driving under Section 42 of the Road Transport Act 1987 carries a penalty of up to five years' imprisonment and a fine of RM5,000 to RM15,000 upon conviction.
He said existing laws are sufficient to address road rage, with additional action also taken under the Penal Code depending on the offence.
These include Section 323 (causing hurt), Sections 324 or 326 (causing hurt using weapons or dangerous means), Section 341 (wrongful restraint), Section 427 (mischief or property damage), and Section 506 (criminal intimidation).
On viral road rage videos, he said swift police action is important to show that all complaints are taken seriously and to strengthen public confidence in enforcement.
However, he said viral videos are only initial information, and each case is investigated professionally using CCTV footage, dashcam recordings, witness statements and digital forensic evidence before action is taken.
He sad swift and consistent enforcement acts as a deterrent, while the public is encouraged to cooperate by submitting dashcam or CCTV footage when witnessing offences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Huge crowds gather as Khamenei funeral ceremonies begin in Iran [WATCH]
Article:
TEHERAN: Funeral ceremonies for Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei officially began on Saturday, state television reported, an event drawing thousands and intended to serve as a show of strength to the Islamic republic's foes.
Iranian authorities say they anticipate between 15 and 20 million participants in Teheran alone over the next three days for tributes to the man who ran the country for three-and-a-half decades.
Six days of funeral ceremonies are planned to commemorate Khamenei, who ruled the Islamic republic as its number one from 1989 until his killing aged 86 on the first day of the US-Israeli war with Iran on Feb 28.
The events will be scrutinised in particular for any signs of Khamenei's son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei, who was named supreme leader a week after the killing of his father but is yet to appear in public.
Thousands of mourners carrying red banners – a symbol of vengeance – gathered in the courtyard of Teheran's vast Grand Mosalla religious complex waiting for the arrival of Khamenei's coffin, an AFP journalist witnessed.
Chants of "death to America" and "revenge, revenge" echoed at the venue.
Another AFP journalist saw mourners walking several kilometres to reach the venue. Hundreds of supporters of the Islamic republic were already waiting on Friday evening outside the Grand Mosalla.
"We want to say a final goodbye to our leader, which is why waiting like this isn't painful or difficult for us," Somayye Hamedi told AFP as she waited.
Significant security measures have been imposed, with roads blocked and airspace expected to be closed for what is set to be the largest-scale public event in Iran since the burial of Khamenei's predecessor Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.
- 'FINAL GOODBYE' -
The coffin will lie in state until Monday, when a procession will pass through Teheran. On Tuesday it will move to the clerical hub of Qom, before moving on Wednesday to cities holy to Shia Muslims in neighbouring Iraq, then proceeding for burial on Thursday in Khamenei's home city of Mashhad in northeastern Iran.
Officials who survived the war showed their grief and displayed a united front on Friday, with parliament speaker and top negotiator in the US talks Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf visibly tearful.
Top Iranian officials paying their respects included Ahmad Vahidi, who was named chief of the powerful Revolutionary Guards after his predecessor was killed in the same strikes that killed Khamenei but had not been seen since.
The coming days will be closely watched for signs of Mojtaba Khamenei, who has communicated only by written statements and is said to have been wounded in the same strikes, though the extent of his injuries was never made clear.
Other relatives killed in the strikes will also be buried, including Ali Khamenei's infant granddaughter.
International guests paying respects Friday included Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, whose country has been mediating between Iran and the US, and Russian ex-president Dmitry Medvedev, now deputy head of the Russian security council, who attended on behalf of President Vladimir Putin.
Hamas and Lebanese Shia group Hizbollah, both backed by Teheran, were also represented, as was the Taliban government in Afghanistan.
- 'CALL FOR VENGEANCE' -
After five weeks of fighting, the Middle East conflict is on hold following an initial accord between Iran and the US. But Iranian officials have warned Teheran is ready to resume fighting if needed.
"The nation's call for vengeance must ring in the ears of the whole world," said Ghalibaf, who urged Iranians to turn out en masse.
Army chief Amir Hatami vowed Israel and the US "will pay for the blood of the martyred leader and all the nation's martyrs."
But authorities will also want the event to go smoothly, deeply aware of the risk of crowd crushes, which have marred similar events in the past, with TV broadcasting guidelines on how to stay safe.
With temperatures well over 35 degrees Celsius expected over the next few days in Teheran, tankers were stationed to spray water on the roads to cool down attendees.
Ahead of the ceremonies, AFP correspondents reported that Teheran was quieter than usual, with many normally busy streets free of Teheran's notorious traffic.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Jaya's Pakatan debutant bets on youth vote to shape state’s future
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Johor Jaya candidate Lee Wern Yiing believes young voters will play a decisive role in shaping Johor’s future at the upcoming state election.
The 30-year-old first-time candidate said the younger generation’s votes would be crucial in determining the state’s direction.
“Young voters need to be aware of this and, hopefully, they can play a role as they are more exposed to information and technology.
“I am confident that the Johor Jaya voters, including the young, will be wise in making their decision,” she said when met briefly during a campaign walkabout in Taman Johor Jaya here today.
Lee, who is also Johor DAP Socialist Youth (DAPSY) chief, is no stranger to the constituency, having spent the past eight years handling cases in Johor Jaya.
She said that if elected, she would bring the voices of young people, residents’ concerns and the aspirations of the community to the Johor state assembly.
Lee began her political journey in 2018 when she joined the team of former Johor Jaya assemblyman and state executive councillor Liow Cai Tung as a special officer.
She has since become a familiar face in Johor Jaya through her community work over the past two election cycles.
The Johor Jaya state seat, located in Johor Bahru, is a growing constituency comprising residential neighbourhoods and light industrial areas.
Like many urban constituencies in Johor Bahru, it faces issues related to infrastructure, traffic congestion and local economic development.
DAP, first under Pakatan Rakyat and later PH, has held the semi-urban seat since 2013. However, it is regarded as a marginal constituency after the coalition secured only narrow majorities in previous elections.
Johor Jaya will see a four-cornered contest involving Lee, Barisan Nasional’s Chan San San, Parti Bersama Malaysia’s Lau Yi Leong and independent candidate Lim Hun Peaw.
Polling for the Johor state election is set for July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.

Title: Malaysia on track for 100pc internet coverage in populated areas by 2027
Article:
BATU PAHAT, July 4 — The government through the Ministry of Communications is targeting 100 per cent Internet access in populated areas nationwide by next year through the implementation of the National Digital Network (JENDELA).
Ministry of Communications secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah said that as of Dec 31, broadband coverage in such areas had reached 99.71 per cent, which reflected the high commitment of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).
He said that before the implementation of Jendela, internet access nationwide was only around 90 per cent, but through the latest phase of the plan, MCMC will focus on Internet access up to 4G and 5G.
“We, the ministry and MCMC, will continue to expand access to 4G and strengthen 5G, including in rural areas, which is being implemented through cooperation and infrastructure sharing between telecommunications companies.
“With this method, it can accelerate the expansion of coverage and improve the quality of services to users,” he told Bernama after watching the live broadcast of the Fifa 2026 World Cup match in conjunction with JAPEN Arena Penggerak Komuniti (JAGO) programme in Kampung Istana, here today.
Also present to watch the match were Information Department (JAPEN) director-general Erwin Khairul Ahmad and Kampung Istana Village Development and Security Committee (JPKK) chairman Abd Jalil Ali.
According to Abdul Halim, the expansion of good Internet access can launch various sectors including education and the economy, especially in rural and interior areas.
Meanwhile, Erwin Khairul said the JAGO programme is a continuation of the ‘wayang pacak’ programme which is now being modernised, but its role is maintained as a delivery platform information and government policies.
He said that currently, there are four multi-function vehicle (MFV) units operating nationwide and his party plans to increase the asset to 16 units in the future, besides targeting each state to have at least one unit of the vehicle.
“We will always move throughout the country including delivering government initiatives such as targeted diesel subsidies, exemption from ATM withdrawal charges and book vouchers for students.
“In addition, we JAPEN also collaborate with the National Film Development Corporation of Malaysia (Finas) to broadcast local films to the community,” he said.
Meanwhile, Abd Jalil said as the host of the JAGO programme, his party was excited and at the same time proud when the village was selected as the Kampung Angkat Madani (KAM) of the Ministry of Communications 2026.
“Through this KAM programme, the residents here will benefit when the public hall built in 1950 will be upgraded with an allocation of RM400,000.
“In addition, another RM100,000 will be used by the local community for joint programmes and activities, including welfare contributions to the needy,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Anwar says Bumiputera empowerment is national priority, not just agencies’ responsibility
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — The Bumiputera empowerment agenda is no longer the sole responsibility of specific agencies but a national priority to be pursued by all ministries, government agencies and government-linked companies, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said government agencies must align their policies and programmes to ensure the Bumiputera development agenda is implemented comprehensively.
According to Anwar, the government has introduced the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35), with implementation regularly monitored and all ministries and agencies required to report their progress.
“All MADANI Government agencies are responsible for delivering the nation’s policies. Those policies are aimed at driving economic growth while ensuring fair and equitable distribution,” he said when officiating the SPaRK 2026: Business Transformation programme organised by Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Bhd (PUNB) here today.
The Prime Minister said the government has no plans to establish a new Bumiputera agency, opting instead to strengthen existing institutions and ensure that all ministries and agencies play their respective roles.
Anwar, who also serves as Finance Minister, said the approach would accelerate policy implementation while preventing overlapping functions among agencies.
“I think continuing with the old way of doing things while expecting better results is not going to work.
“For that reason, I am not inclined to establish a new Bumiputera agency, but rather to ensure existing agencies are strengthened,” he said.
Meanwhile, Anwar said the government will continue to focus on driving national economic growth without neglecting fair wealth distribution to the people, emphasising that economic development must be implemented in an inclusive manner, so that all segments of society continue to benefit.
“We do not prevent anyone from working hard to drive economic growth, whether in artificial intelligence (AI), quantum computing, the digital economy or the energy transition. However, at the same time, we must ensure that distribution is fair,” he said.
Anwar also said the government is adopting a “raising the ceiling” approach to boost the country’s economic competitiveness, while at the same time “raising the floor” to support those still in need. — Bernama

Title: BN-Pakatan cooperation remains intact despite Johor campaign heat, says Zahid
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, July 4 — Barisan Nasional (BN) enjoys a very good relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, with cooperation between the two political coalitions remaining strong despite the heat of the Johor state election campaign.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi stressed that the leadership of both BN and PH has consistently demonstrated professionalism and political maturity, placing the national agenda above all other considerations.
“Cooperation between BN and PH is very good. Let me confirm that it is indeed very good, and we remain highly professional.
“We distinguish between national issues, which we must address together as members of the Cabinet, and issues at the state level, for example,” he said after officiating the RISDA Agro Majestic event in Bandar Tenggara today.
Commenting on the current Johor state election campaign, Ahmad Zahid said both political coalitions remain committed to preserving harmony within the Unity Government by adopting a mature approach to election campaigning.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also Umno president, said the political strategies of both sides are based on healthy competition without resorting to excessive provocation.
“Among the approaches we have adopted is that we do not attack one another, even though we are contesting in the same arena, particularly in Johor. The same approach will apply in Negeri Sembilan, but we will not attack below the belt.
“If anyone from either side slips up, we should de-escalate the situation. Most importantly, democracy must be upheld and practised in the best possible manner,” he said.
He said the winning formula in today’s political landscape no longer depends on a party’s ability to attack or discredit its opponents. Instead, voters assess the quality and merits of the candidates put forward.
He cited as examples the healthy competition among candidates with professional backgrounds, including in the medical field, as well as former ministers.
“We should promote our respective candidates, such as Dr Naqib Ghazali in Panti and Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba in Pasir Raja, who is a medical specialist and a former health minister. These are the candidates we should be promoting, rather than attacking other parties or their candidates,” he said.
A total of 172 candidates have been confirmed to contest the Johor state election, which is scheduled to be held on July 11, while early voting has been set for July 7.
For the latest news on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/. — Bernama

Title: Government’s RM429m allocation since 2023 ensures Johor’s enforcement agencies thrive
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — The Home Ministry (KDN) has allocated more than RM429 million since 2023 to strengthen the welfare of personnel and enhance the operational capabilities of enforcement agencies in Johor.
Its minister, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, said the allocation covers three key agencies, namely the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM), and the Malaysian Prisons Department.
He said investing in personnel welfare goes beyond providing better facilities, describing it as a strategic effort to ensure enforcement officers can carry out their duties more efficiently, safely, and effectively in safeguarding public security.
